hushProfile.name: Brave supplement
hushProfile.description: Narrow companion for Brave Shields users. Covers the gaps Shields doesn't close: first-party telemetry subdomains on major sites, session-replay vendor blocks at the script-origin layer (catches bundled copies Shields' URL lists miss), and site-specific custom-element cleanup (Reddit promoted posts, Amazon ad iframes, GitHub telemetry collector). Does NOT duplicate Shields' work (no generic EasyList patterns, no fingerprint spoofs - Shields farbles those better). Load on top of Shields with zero expected conflicts.

spoof.comment: navigator.sendBeacon returns true without firing. The API's literal purpose is page-unload telemetry; no user-side benefit exists. Killed site-wide.
navigator.clipboard.readText rejects with NotAllowedError (same DOMException the browser raises when the user denies the permission prompt). Kills coupon-sniffing and paste-in analytics.
Bluetooth.requestDevice rejects with NotFoundError. Delete this line if you genuinely use a site's Bluetooth integration (maker-space, physical-web dashboards).
USB.requestDevice rejects. Delete this line if you use WebUSB for devices (very rare on general browsing).
HID.requestDevice rejects. Delete this line if you use WebHID (gamepads via WebHID, specialized peripherals).
Serial.requestPort rejects. Delete this line if you use WebSerial for hardware (Arduino IDE web, etc).

neuter.comment: Hotjar: deny interaction-listener registration at document_start. Catches bundled copies that don't load from hotjar.com but still call addEventListener from Hotjar-origin code.
Hotjar static CDN - alternate origin for the same library.
FullStory: same shape as Hotjar. Session-replay listener density is the signal.
Microsoft Clarity - free session-replay bundled in enterprise analytics packages.
PostHog has an opt-in session-recording feature; if the site enabled it, neuter stops the capture regardless.

silence.comment: PostHog exfil fallback: if Neuter misses the listener (inline or non-prototype capture), Silence kills the outbound fetch/XHR/beacon from PostHog-origin code.

block.comment: Reddit's first-party event-telemetry pipeline. Receives sendBeacon events for impressions, dwell time, feed-position feedback. Not on any public blocklist - first-party subdomains aren't. Blocking it has no user-visible impact: all user-facing Reddit features use oauth.reddit.com / gql.reddit.com / www.reddit.com/api/*.

remove.comment: Reddit-authored custom element for games promotion in the feed. No public cosmetic-filter list targets custom elements this narrow.
Brand Affiliate / promoted posts. The :has() selector targets the post only when it contains the is-post-commercial-communication flag - safer than class-matching Reddit's utility classes.
Reddit's algorithmic community recommendations inserted into the feed. These aren't ads per se but they're anti-user algorithmic insertions; remove on principle.

block.comment: Amazon's first-party behavior-telemetry pipeline. Not on public lists.
North America shard of the same pipeline.

remove.comment: Amazon's homepage ad iframes use IDs prefixed with ape_ (a-p-e = Amazon Programmatic Exchange). Verified via behavioral-detector hidden-iframe suggestion.
Programmatic ad iframes loaded from Amazon's ad-exchange CDN. Distinct from the ape_ iframes; both appear as hidden-iframe suggestions.

block.comment: GitHub's first-party sendBeacon telemetry collector. ~12 beacons per minute of normal browsing. User-facing features run on api.github.com / *.github.io / *.githubusercontent.com; collector is strictly telemetry.
First-party analytics subdomain. Not in Brave's URL stripping.
Twitter's analytics pipeline still lives on the old analytics.twitter.com host even when served from x.com.
LinkedIn's first-party ad pixel endpoint. Loads from linkedin.com so no cross-site filter list blocks it.
